basmala: In the name of God, the All-Merciful, the Compassionate.
intro: al-Jumuʿa — “The Congregation” (Friday) — celebrates the sending of an “unlettered” Prophet among the unlettered, who recites, purifies, and teaches. Qushayrī opens with a basmala on disclosure (tajallī): the mighty Name that, when it manifests to the servant's heart by the attribute of Its beauty, gathers all his thoughts upon the carpet of Its generosity, so that he no longer scatters into any other than Him; and when it manifests to his secret by the attribute of Its majesty, his whole being folds and is absorbed into Its existence, until he perceives nothing of the splendours of his world nor the terrors of his Hereafter. He meditates on the Prophet's unletteredness as an absolute purity from all human learning: He stripped him of all effort to learn, then said: “He taught you what you did not know.” The sūra rebukes those who carry the Torah without carrying it — “like the donkey loaded with books”; it challenges those who claim God's friendship to wish for death; and it commands hastening to the Friday call, abandoning trade, for “what is with God is better than diversion and trade.”
themes: the sending of the unlettered Prophet
carrying the Book without carrying it

versets.ar: يُسَبِّحُ لِلَّهِ مَا فِي السَّمَاوَاتِ وَمَا فِي الْأَرْضِ الْمَلِكِ الْقُدُّوسِ الْعَزِيزِ الْحَكِيمِ
versets.tr: yusabbiḥu lillāhi mā fī s-samāwāti wa-mā fī l-arḍi l-maliki l-quddūsi l-ʿazīzi l-ḥakīmi
versets.trad: All that is in the heavens and all that is on the earth glorifies God, the King, the All-Holy, the Mighty, the Wise.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: in the seas of the Real's tawḥīd sail the secrets of the people of realization — and their sea has no shore: once they have entered it, there is no exit nor respite; their hands have seized in it the jewels of tafrīd and set them in the crown of gnosis, to wear it on the Day of the encounter. On al-Quddūs (the All-Holy), he lays a key rule: man has access only to the knowledge of realities by way of transcendence and to the contemplation of acts — as for attaining the reality of His ipseity, the absolute Self-Sufficiency (ṣamadiyya) is too sublime to be reached.

mots.ar: يُسَبِّحُ لِلَّهِ مَا فِي السَّمَاوَاتِ وَمَا فِي الْأَرْضِ
mots.tr: yusabbiḥu lillāhi mā fī s-samāwāti wa-mā fī l-arḍ
mots.racine: س ب ح
mots.sens: all in the heavens and earth glorifies God

mots.racine: م ل ك / ق د س
mots.sens: the King, the All-Holy

mots.racine: ع ز ز / ح ك م
mots.sens: the Mighty, the Wise

versets.ar: هُوَ الَّذِي بَعَثَ فِي الْأُمِّيِّينَ رَسُولًا مِّنْهُمْ يَتْلُو عَلَيْهِمْ آيَاتِهِ وَيُزَكِّيهِمْ وَيُعَلِّمُهُمُ الْكِتَابَ وَالْحِكْمَةَ وَإِن كَانُوا مِن قَبْلُ لَفِي ضَلَالٍ مُّبِينٍ
versets.tr: huwa lladhī baʿatha fī l-ummiyyīna rasūlan minhum yatlū ʿalayhim āyātihi wa-yuzakkīhim wa-yuʿallimuhumu l-kitāba wa-l-ḥikmata wa-in kānū min qablu la-fī ḍalālin mubīnin
versets.trad: It is He who raised up among the unlettered a messenger from themselves, who recites to them His verses, purifies them, and teaches them the Book and Wisdom, though before they were in manifest error,
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī meditates on unletteredness (ummiyya) as a willed purity: He stripped him of all effort to learn and all seeking, then raised him up among them and made appear upon him qualities that surpassed all others. As He had orphaned him at the beginning, of his father and mother, then sheltered him by His gentleness — which was more perfect — so He freed him from the effort of learning, yet said: “He taught you what you did not know.” The Prophet holds his knowledge from no human master: his science is a pure gift.

mots.racine: ب ع ث / أ م م / ر س ل
mots.sens: He raised among the unlettered a messenger from them
mots.def: baʿatha: to send, raise up; ummiyyūn: the unlettered.

mots.racine: ت ل و / ز ك و
mots.sens: he recites His signs to them and purifies them
mots.def: talā: to recite; zakkā: to purify.

mots.racine: ع ل م / ك ت ب / ح ك م
mots.sens: he teaches them the Book and Wisdom

mots.racine: ض ل ل / ب ي ن
mots.sens: in manifest error

versets.ar: وَآخَرِينَ مِنْهُمْ لَمَّا يَلْحَقُوا بِهِمْ ۚ وَهُوَ الْعَزِيزُ الْحَكِيمُ
versets.tr: wa-ākharīna minhum lammā yalḥaqū bihim wa-huwa l-ʿazīzu l-ḥakīmu
versets.trad: and to others of them who have not yet joined them. He is the Mighty, the Wise!
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: He raised him among the unlettered, and among others than them — the non-Arabs (ʿajam) and those to come, until the Day of Resurrection; so he is sent to all humanity. The Prophet's mission is bounded by neither a people nor an age: “those who have not yet joined them” are all the generations to come, embraced in advance in his sending.

mots.racine: أ خ ر
mots.sens: and others of them

mots.racine: ل ح ق
mots.sens: who have not yet joined them
mots.def: laḥiqa: to join, catch up with.

mots.racine: ع ز ز / ح ك م
mots.sens: the Mighty, the Wise

versets.ar: ذَٰلِكَ فَضْلُ اللَّهِ يُؤْتِيهِ مَن يَشَاءُ ۚ وَاللَّهُ ذُو الْفَضْلِ الْعَظِيمِ
versets.tr: dhālika faḍlu llāhi yuʾtīhi man yashāʾu wa-llāhu dhū l-faḍli l-ʿaẓīmi
versets.trad: Such is the grace of God! He gives it to whom He wills. And God is the Possessor of immense grace!
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: grace (faḍl) is what is not owed; the owed is obligation, not grace. He refutes two errors about prophethood: a reply to those who say it is earned by the abundance of the prophet's deeds, and a reply to those who say it accrues to them by their predisposed clay (ṭīna). And he gives Sufi senses: grace, here, is the success (tawfīq) to believe; some say: intimacy with God (uns) — and the servant, when he finds intimacy, forgets everything; some say: the cutting off of secondary causes.

mots.racine: ف ض ل
mots.sens: such is the grace of God

mots.racine: أ ت ي / ش ي أ
mots.sens: He gives it to whom He wills
mots.def: ātā: to give; shāʾa: to will.

mots.racine: ف ض ل / ع ظ م
mots.sens: God is the Possessor of immense grace
mots.def: dhū al-faḍl: holder of grace; ʿaẓīm: immense.

versets.ar: مَثَلُ الَّذِينَ حُمِّلُوا التَّوْرَاةَ ثُمَّ لَمْ يَحْمِلُوهَا كَمَثَلِ الْحِمَارِ يَحْمِلُ أَسْفَارًا ۚ بِئْسَ مَثَلُ الْقَوْمِ الَّذِينَ كَذَّبُوا بِآيَاتِ اللَّهِ ۚ وَاللَّهُ لَا يَهْدِي الْقَوْمَ الظَّالِمِينَ
versets.tr: mathalu lladhīna ḥummilū t-tawrāta thumma lam yaḥmilūhā ka-mathali l-ḥimāri yaḥmilu asfāran biʾsa mathalu l-qawmi lladhīna kadhdhabū bi-āyāti llāhi wa-llāhu lā yahdī l-qawma ẓ-ẓālimīna
versets.trad: Those who were charged with the Torah and then did not carry it are like the donkey carrying books. Wretched is the example of the people who denied the signs of God. God does not guide the unjust people!
themes: carrying the Book without carrying it
versets.commentaire: “Then did not carry it,” Qushayrī explains, means: then did not put it into practice. And he extends the warning, by allusion, far beyond the People of the Book: joined to them in this threat are those marked by blind imitation (taqlīd) in any field whatever — in the science of fundamentals, in what rests on the proofs of reason, and, in this Path, in what rests on the spiritual descents (munāzalāt). To carry knowledge without living it is to be the donkey carrying books while receiving nothing of them.

mots.racine: ح م ل / ت و ر
mots.sens: charged with the Torah then did not carry it
mots.def: ḥummila: to be charged; ḥamala: to carry.

mots.racine: ح م ر / س ف ر
mots.sens: like the donkey carrying books
mots.def: ḥimār: donkey; asfār: books (plural of sifr).

mots.racine: ب أ س / ك ذ ب
mots.sens: wretched is the example of the deniers
mots.def: biʾsa: verb of blame; kadhdhaba: to deny.

mots.racine: ه د ي / ظ ل م
mots.sens: He does not guide the unjust

versets.ar: قُلْ يَا أَيُّهَا الَّذِينَ هَادُوا إِن زَعَمْتُمْ أَنَّكُمْ أَوْلِيَاءُ لِلَّهِ مِن دُونِ النَّاسِ فَتَمَنَّوُا الْمَوْتَ إِن كُنتُمْ صَادِقِينَ
versets.tr: qul yā ayyuhā lladhīna hādū in zaʿamtum annakum awliyāʾu lillāhi min dūni n-nāsi fa-tamannawu l-mawta in kuntum ṣādiqīna
versets.trad: Say: “O you who are Jews! If you claim to be the friends of God to the exclusion of [other] people, then wish for death, if you are truthful!”
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī makes of this challenge a sign of prophethood: this is among his miracles: that he turned their hearts from wishing for death until this term proved his truthfulness. And he draws the law of love: among the marks of love is ardent longing for the Beloved; and since one reaches His encounter only through death, to wish for it is necessarily its condition. Whoever truly loves God desires death as the gate of encounter — and their refusal betrays the falsehood of their claim.

mots.racine: ه و د
mots.sens: O you who are Jews

mots.racine: ز ع م / و ل ي
mots.sens: if you claim to be the friends of God
mots.def: zaʿama: to claim; awliyāʾ: friends, allies.

mots.racine: م ن ي / م و ت / ص د ق
mots.sens: wish for death if you are truthful

versets.ar: وَلَا يَتَمَنَّوْنَهُ أَبَدًا بِمَا قَدَّمَتْ أَيْدِيهِمْ ۚ وَاللَّهُ عَلِيمٌ بِالظَّالِمِينَ
versets.tr: wa-lā yatamannawnahu abadan bimā qaddamat aydīhim wa-llāhu ʿalīmun bi-ẓ-ẓālimīna
versets.trad: But they will never wish for it, because of what their hands have sent ahead. And God knows the unjust!
versets.commentaire: The refusal to wish for death discloses their awareness of their deeds: they know what their hands have sent ahead. Qushayrī: the challenge of the prior verse was twofold — a command (“wish for death”) and a prediction (“they will never wish for it”); and it came to pass exactly as foretold. Whoever fears the encounter carries within himself the verdict of his own deeds; and God, ʿalīm, knows perfectly what the heart of the unjust conceals.

mots.racine: م ن ي
mots.sens: they will never wish for it

mots.racine: ق د م / ي د ي
mots.sens: because of what their hands sent ahead
mots.def: qaddama: to send ahead, do beforehand.

mots.racine: ع ل م / ظ ل م
mots.sens: God knows the unjust

versets.ar: قُلْ إِنَّ الْمَوْتَ الَّذِي تَفِرُّونَ مِنْهُ فَإِنَّهُ مُلَاقِيكُمْ ۖ ثُمَّ تُرَدُّونَ إِلَىٰ عَالِمِ الْغَيْبِ وَالشَّهَادَةِ فَيُنَبِّئُكُم بِمَا كُنتُمْ تَعْمَلُونَ
versets.tr: qul inna l-mawta lladhī tafirrūna minhu fa-innahu mulāqīkum thumma turaddūna ilā ʿālimi l-ghaybi wa-sh-shahādati fa-yunabbiʾukum bimā kuntum taʿmalūna
versets.trad: Say: “The death you flee will surely meet you! Then you will be brought back to the Knower of the hidden and the manifest, and He will inform you of what you used to do.”
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: death is a settled decree. He cites the hadith: “Whoever dislikes the encounter with God, God dislikes his encounter.” And he extends the image: death is a bridge, and the goal is with God; whoever has not lived as a pure man, let him at least die an elegant one (ẓarīf). Fleeing death is vain, for it is the inevitable passage to the One who knows the hidden and the manifest, before whom every deed will be disclosed.

mots.racine: م و ت / ف ر ر
mots.sens: the death you flee

mots.racine: ل ق ي
mots.sens: it will meet you

mots.racine: ع ل م / غ ي ب / ش ه د
mots.sens: the Knower of the hidden and the manifest

mots.racine: ن ب أ / ع م ل
mots.sens: He will inform you of what you did

versets.ar: يَا أَيُّهَا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا إِذَا نُودِيَ لِلصَّلَاةِ مِن يَوْمِ الْجُمُعَةِ فَاسْعَوْا إِلَىٰ ذِكْرِ اللَّهِ وَذَرُوا الْبَيْعَ ۚ ذَٰلِكُمْ خَيْرٌ لَّكُمْ إِن كُنتُمْ تَعْلَمُونَ
versets.tr: yā ayyuhā lladhīna āmanū idhā nūdiya li-ṣ-ṣalāti min yawmi l-jumuʿati fa-sʿaw ilā dhikri llāhi wa-dharū l-bayʿa dhālikum khayrun lakum in kuntum taʿlamūna
versets.trad: O you who believe! When the call to prayer is made on the day of Congregation, hasten to the remembrance of God and leave off trade! That is better for you, if only you knew!
versets.commentaire: The verse that gives the sūra its name (al-jumuʿa). Qushayrī opens two levels: some hear “leave off trade” in the literal sense — to cease all commerce with creatures; others add a deeper sense: to cease occupying oneself with the goods of this world (aʿrāḍ), to forget all designs, save the embrace of the Command. So there is one who hastens to the remembrance of God (dhikr Allāh), and one who hastens to God Himself: the former go to the remembrance openly, the latter go to God secret to secret.

mots.ar: إِذَا نُودِيَ لِلصَّلَاةِ مِن يَوْمِ الْجُمُعَةِ
mots.tr: idhā nūdiya li-ṣ-ṣalāti min yawmi l-jumuʿa
mots.racine: ن د و / ص ل و / ج م ع
mots.sens: when the call to prayer is made on Friday
mots.def: nūdiya: to be called; jumuʿa: Friday (the day of gathering).

mots.racine: س ع ي / ذ ك ر
mots.sens: hasten to the remembrance of God

mots.racine: و ذ ر / ب ي ع
mots.sens: leave off trade
mots.def: wadhara: to leave; bayʿ: sale, trade.

mots.racine: خ ي ر
mots.sens: that is better for you

versets.ar: فَإِذَا قُضِيَتِ الصَّلَاةُ فَانتَشِرُوا فِي الْأَرْضِ وَابْتَغُوا مِن فَضْلِ اللَّهِ وَاذْكُرُوا اللَّهَ كَثِيرًا لَّعَلَّكُمْ تُفْلِحُونَ
versets.tr: fa-idhā quḍiyati ṣ-ṣalātu fa-ntashirū fī l-arḍi wa-btaghū min faḍli llāhi wa-dhkurū llāha kathīran laʿallakum tufliḥūna
versets.trad: When the prayer is finished, disperse through the land and seek the bounty of God, and remember God much, that you may prosper.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī distinguishes the ranks: only he who has a trade to return to, or a matter to pursue, disperses. But he who has neither matter nor refuge — where would he return to? “Seek the bounty of God” is said to one who still has a desire; but he who has grown still, free of demands, from whom the very disease of seeking has been removed — what has he to do with seeking what he does not want and is not enslaved to? For the common, dispersal and gain; for the elite, only the dhikr remains.

mots.racine: ق ض ي / ص ل و
mots.sens: when the prayer is finished
mots.def: quḍiya: to be completed; ṣalāt: prayer.

mots.racine: ن ش ر / أ ر ض
mots.sens: disperse through the land

mots.racine: ب غ ي / ف ض ل
mots.sens: seek the bounty of God

mots.racine: ذ ك ر / ف ل ح
mots.sens: remember God much, that you may prosper
mots.def: dhakara: to remember; aflaḥa: to prosper.

versets.ar: وَإِذَا رَأَوْا تِجَارَةً أَوْ لَهْوًا انفَضُّوا إِلَيْهَا وَتَرَكُوكَ قَائِمًا ۚ قُلْ مَا عِندَ اللَّهِ خَيْرٌ مِّنَ اللَّهْوِ وَمِنَ التِّجَارَةِ ۚ وَاللَّهُ خَيْرُ الرَّازِقِينَ
versets.tr: wa-idhā raʾaw tijāratan aw lahwan infaḍḍū ilayhā wa-tarakūka qāʾiman qul mā ʿinda llāhi khayrun mina l-lahwi wa-mina t-tijārati wa-llāhu khayru r-rāziqīna
versets.trad: But when they catch sight of trade or diversion, they rush off to it and leave you standing! Say: “What is with God is better than diversion and trade!” And God is the best of providers!
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī opposes two kinds of men: he whom the perils of things have captivated answers every call that diversion holds out or inadvertence imposes; but he whom the power of the Reality possesses does not deviate from the Presence nor turn away in the state of contemplation. And he unfolds “what is with God”: for the worshippers and renunciants, tomorrow, it is better than what they obtained here in cash; and for the gnostics, in cash, it is the influxes of the hearts and the flashes of the Reality — better than all the beginner still hopes for, in this world and the next.

mots.ar: وَإِذَا رَأَوْا تِجَارَةً أَوْ لَهْوًا انفَضُّوا إِلَيْهَا
mots.tr: wa-idhā raʾaw tijāratan aw lahwan infaḍḍū ilayhā
mots.racine: ر أ ي / ت ج ر / ل ه و / ف ض ض
mots.sens: when they see trade or diversion, they rush off to it
mots.def: lahw: diversion; infaḍḍa: to rush off, disperse.

mots.racine: ت ر ك / ق و م
mots.sens: and leave you standing

mots.ar: مَا عِندَ اللَّهِ خَيْرٌ مِّنَ اللَّهْوِ وَمِنَ التِّجَارَةِ
mots.tr: mā ʿinda llāhi khayrun mina l-lahwi wa-mina t-tijāra
mots.racine: خ ي ر / ل ه و / ت ج ر
mots.sens: what is with God is better than diversion and trade

mots.racine: ر ز ق
mots.sens: God is the best of providers
